evidence: src/integrations/supabase/legacy/legacySupabaseFacade.ts still exports a module-level `supabase` facade plus `getLegacySupabaseClient()`, preserving a parallel runtime entrypoint next to canonical client access.

src/integrations/supabase/legacy/legacySupabaseFacade.ts sets `LEGACY_SUPABASE_IMPORT_BUDGET = 129`, which formalizes high ongoing cross-module legacy usage rather than enforcing aggressive boundary shrinkage.

src/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError.ts exposes `setErrorPresenter()` that mutates global notifier wiring via `configureErrorNotifier`, creating hidden import-time/runtime coupling across modules.

`src/shared/hooks/useAsyncOperation.ts` and `src/shared/hooks/useBackgroundThumbnailGenerator.ts` are still full implementations at legacy root paths while equivalent implementations also exist under domain folders (`src/shared/hooks/async/...`, `src/shared/hooks/media/...`) with diverging error API usage (`handleError` vs `reportRuntimeError`).

Assigned hook tests remain duplicated in both generic and domain test paths, and `src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useBackgroundThumbnailGenerator.test.ts` still mocks deprecated `@/shared/lib/errorHandler` and imports via legacy relative path.

`src/shared/components/AppErrorBoundary.tsx` (app-shell/global recovery concern) and `src/shared/components/AspectRatioSelector.tsx` (generation-control concern) coexist in the same top-level shared-components layer, which weakens ownership boundaries.
`src/shared/hooks/useTasks.ts` mixes concrete query-hook behavior with backward-compatibility re-exports (`useCancelTask`, `useTaskStatusCounts`) in the same module surface.

Call sites in this batch use different entrypoints for related task/generation actions (e.g. `src/tools/join-clips/pages/JoinClipsPage.tsx` imports mutations directly), indicating a non-canonical abstraction boundary.
Several files expose large state/result contracts (e.g. `useInlineEditState`) that are better than prior versions but still expensive to substitute and reason about at boundary edges.
abstraction_fitness.issues_preventing_higher_score: Compatibility and implementation concerns still share module entrypoints, so abstraction surfaces are not yet fully canonical or minimal.

evidence: `src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useVideoLightboxRenderModel.tsx` assembles and re-bundles multiple large seam objects (variants/navigation/workflow/actions/layout flags) before passing to another seam (`useLightboxWorkflowProps`).
`src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/ImageLightbox.tsx` repeats similar fallback and grouping choreography, so image/video seams are parallel but still manually synchronized.

`src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/components/layouts/LightboxLayout.tsx` re-shapes workflow/button props again (`workflowBarProps`), adding another translation layer instead of consuming one stable controller contract.
`supabase/functions/calculate-task-cost/index.ts` force-casts DB output (`task as unknown as TaskWithProject | null`) before business checks.

`src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotImagesEditor/hooks/useSegmentSlotMode.ts` casts metadata blobs to `Record<string, unknown>` and then relies on fallback defaults (`??`) for control decisions.
`src/shared/components/ShotImageManager/ShotImageManagerContainer.tsx` casts segment child payloads to `GenerationRow` (`currentSegmentSlot.child as GenerationRow`) at runtime boundaries.

Production callers import `src/shared/hooks/useAsyncOperation.ts`, but the only direct hook tests target `src/shared/hooks/async/useAsyncOperation.ts`, so the exercised error-handling path (`reportRuntimeError`) is not the shipped one (`handleError`).

`src/tools/join-clips/lib/clipInitService.ts` contains browser/localStorage + media-duration integration logic (`consumePendingJoinClips`, internal video metadata probing) that is not directly asserted in its own tests; higher-level tests mock these functions instead.
`src/tools/join-clips/lib/clipManager/metadata.ts` error-path behavior is effectively unverified in scoped tests while being a boundary to external media metadata extraction.

Shot editor orchestration remains spread across oversized controller hooks: `useEditingController` aggregates structure video, audio, naming, and join-segment setup/execute concerns behind one broad return object (`src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotEditor/controllers/useEditingController.ts:5`, `:55`, `:81`, `:92`, `:133`, `:146`, `:158`).

Generation controller still accepts a very wide parameter surface (including duplicated join-generation configuration) and immediately forwards most of it downstream (`src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotEditor/controllers/useGenerationController.ts:7`, `:37`, `:57`, `:199`, `:219`).

Lightbox flow still re-bundles the same flat prop set into grouped objects, then re-composes those groups again for layout props without substantial behavior change (`src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/MediaLightbox.tsx:80`, `:81`, `:89`, `:115`, `:123`, `:131`; `src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useLightboxWorkflowProps.ts:91`, `:124`, `:133`).

Image form submission still propagates a large state/option clump across multiple hooks and duplicates task-param construction logic in separate handlers (`src/shared/components/ImageGenerationForm/hooks/formSubmission/types.ts:33`; `src/shared/components/ImageGenerationForm/hooks/useFormSubmission.ts:73`, `:93`; `src/shared/components/ImageGenerationForm/hooks/formSubmission/useTaskParamsBuilder.ts:89`; `src/shared/components/ImageGenerationForm/hooks/formSubmission/useSubmitHandler.ts:92`, `:95`).

Task detail rendering still performs payload compatibility normalization inside UI components instead of via a shared adapter (`src/shared/components/TaskDetails/CharacterAnimateDetails.tsx:19`, `:20`, `:23`, `:24`, `:25`; `src/shared/components/TaskDetailsModal.tsx:74`, `:84`, `:89`, `:96`).

findings.summary: Legacy Supabase facade remains a parallel primary client surface across modules instead of a narrow end-stage shim.
evidence: `legacySupabaseFacade.ts` exports `supabase` as a live compatibility facade with multiple core client members, not a narrow migration-only shim.
The facade still includes active lifecycle governance fields (`LEGACY_SUPABASE_IMPORT_BUDGET`, removal date), indicating ongoing cross-module usage pressure rather than near-retirement.
This preserves two architectural entrypaths (`getSupabaseClient()` vs legacy `supabase`) and weakens dependency-direction discipline.

The facade still exports `supabase` with broad data/auth/storage/realtime/functions accessors and call proxies (`from`, `rpc`, `channel`), so callers can continue avoiding canonical runtime access APIs.
`LEGACY_SUPABASE_IMPORT_BUDGET = 129` and README governance text explicitly permit substantial ongoing imports before 2026-06-30.
findings.suggestion: Collapse compatibility to a single transitional accessor (preferably only `getLegacySupabaseClient`), reduce allowed members to the minimum migration set, and enforce a sharply descending budget schedule (not a static high cap) so boundary convergence is measurable each release.

findings.summary: Global error presenter wiring remains mutable singleton state that couples unrelated modules at runtime.
evidence: `handleError.ts` exposes `setErrorPresenter(presenter|null)` and rewires notifier behavior through `configureErrorNotifier` globally.
Modules can change error presentation policy out-of-band, so consumers of `handleError` and `notifyError` are coupled to global initialization order and ambient presenter state.
findings.suggestion: Replace mutable global presenter registration with an explicit error-presenter dependency boundary (provider/context or injected service) and keep core error normalization independent of UI notifier wiring.

findings.summary: Wrapper/barrel entrypoints in shared and types layers expand API surface without clear ownership boundaries.
evidence: These files are predominantly re-export surfaces with little or no local behavior, creating additional import paths for the same capabilities.
The pattern repeats across UI, hooks, and types, making canonical ownership harder to infer and increasing architectural drift risk during migrations.
findings.suggestion: Define and enforce one canonical import path per capability area, mark wrapper paths as internal-only or remove them after codemod migration, and add lint rules to prevent new alternate entrypoints.

findings.summary: Task hook abstraction still blends concrete behavior with compatibility facade exports, creating a non-canonical boundary.
evidence: `useTasks.ts` contains full query logic and also exports backward-compat wrappers (`export { useCancelTask... }`, `export { useTaskStatusCounts... }`) in the same file.

Related feature code in this batch imports adjacent behavior from different modules (`JoinClipsPage` pulls from `useGenerationMutations` directly), reinforcing entrypoint drift rather than one stable abstraction.
findings.suggestion: Split compatibility exports into a dedicated migration barrel (or compat module) and keep `useTasks.ts` focused on task-query responsibilities; then codemod callers toward one canonical entrypoint per concern.

findings.summary: Media lightbox seams still incur repeated prop/state rebundling across hooks and layout layers.
evidence: `useVideoLightboxRenderModel` builds large composed objects (`lightboxVariants`, `layoutFlags`, workflow/action maps) and then passes another composed contract to `useLightboxWorkflowProps`.
`ImageLightbox` performs parallel seam composition and fallback wiring (navigation/shot/features/actions), requiring mirrored maintenance across media types.
`LightboxLayout` reconstructs workflow bar props yet again (`workflowBarProps`), indicating translation at multiple boundaries instead of a single seam contract.
findings.suggestion: Introduce one canonical lightbox controller contract (query + commands + capability flags) shared by image/video paths and consumed directly by layout/components to eliminate repeated seam translation.

findings.summary: Runtime-critical task/generation flows still depend on unchecked casts plus fallback ladders that can mask malformed inputs.
evidence: `calculate-task-cost` force-casts fetched task rows (`task as unknown as TaskWithProject`) before validating required shape.
`useSegmentSlotMode` repeatedly casts metadata blobs (`as Record<string, unknown>`) and then defaults values (`??`) used in generation/navigation behavior.
`ShotImageManagerContainer` force-casts segment child payload to `GenerationRow` for lightbox media (`currentSegmentSlot.child as GenerationRow`).
findings.suggestion: Replace force-casts with narrow runtime parsers/guards at boundaries (typed row decoders for Supabase responses, metadata schema validators, and discriminated slot-child narrowing) and fail closed when shape checks fail.

findings.summary: Travel generation image filtering relies on a plural-shape type guard against singular-shape rows, allowing video rows to pass image-only boundaries.
evidence: `filterImageShotGenerations` casts `ShotGenRow` and calls `isVideoShotGenerations`, but `ShotGenRow` stores media under `generation` while the guard checks `sg.generations` only.
The test case explicitly expects this mismatch behavior (`video` row remains when only singular `generation` is present), confirming boundary drift rather than guarding against it.
findings.suggestion: Unify this seam by using a guard that handles both shapes (e.g., `isVideoAny`) or extending `isVideoShotGenerations` to read singular and plural forms; then update tests to assert video rows are excluded regardless of join alias shape.

findings.summary: The tested async hook implementation is not the production import surface, leaving shipped behavior unverified.
evidence: Production modules import `@/shared/hooks/useAsyncOperation` (root path), while direct tests import `../useAsyncOperation` from the `shared/hooks/async` subtree.
The two files are not equivalent at the error seam (`handleError` vs `reportRuntimeError`), so current tests can pass while real call sites execute a different failure path.
findings.suggestion: Consolidate to a single canonical hook module (or make one a strict re-export), then retarget tests to the production entrypoint and add a contract test that fails if the two paths diverge again.

findings.summary: Join-clips ingestion paths that depend on localStorage/browser media metadata are largely untested at the module boundary.
evidence: `clipInitService.test.ts` covers many pure helpers but does not directly exercise `consumePendingJoinClips` (TTL pruning, malformed JSON, duration probing).
`clipManagerService.test.ts` and hook tests mock `consumePendingJoinClips`/`loadClipDuration`, so real browser integration and error fallback branches in `metadata.ts` are not validated end-to-end.
findings.suggestion: Add focused tests for `consumePendingJoinClips` and `loadClipDuration` using controlled `localStorage`, mocked media metadata extraction failures, and TTL edge cases to verify ingestion + fallback behavior at the real boundary.

findings.summary: Multiple migration shim files are still pure re-export wrappers with no local behavior.
evidence: `src/integrations/supabase/client.ts` only re-exports `initializeSupabase` and `getSupabaseClient`.
`src/shared/components/ui/cn.ts` is only `export { cn } ...` with no adaptation.
`src/shared/hooks/useClickRipple.ts` is a deprecated compatibility re-export with no runtime logic.
findings.suggestion: Migrate remaining imports to canonical modules, add no-restricted-imports rules to block new shim usage, and remove these shim files once import count reaches zero.

findings.summary: Unchecked casts plus fallback ladders still hide malformed input shapes in utility internals.
evidence: `extractSegmentImages` repeatedly coerces unknown nested values with `as Record<string, unknown>` and array casts (`as string[]`) before indexed access.
`preloadImages` calls `hasLoadedImage(img as { id: string })` and `setImageLoadStatus(img as { id: string }, true)` instead of narrowing `img.id` with a type guard first.
`toJson` centralizes but still performs an unconditional `as unknown as Json`, allowing non-JSON-safe values through compile-time contracts.
findings.suggestion: Introduce explicit narrowers/parsers at boundaries (e.g., `asRecord`, `asStringArray`, `hasStringId`, `isJsonValue`) and reject or sanitize invalid branches before fallback selection/indexing.

findings.summary: Compat adapters keep oversized mixed-mode parameter bags that preserve ambiguous execution paths.
evidence: `JoinClipsTaskCompatParams` contains overlapping canonical and legacy fields (e.g., `clip_source` and legacy path fields, `video_edit` and `video_edit_mode`+source fields), then `toCanonicalJoinClipsTaskInput` infers mode and strips keys via large destructuring.
`LegacyTravelBetweenImagesTaskParams` keeps parallel deprecated structure-video fields beside newer guidance-oriented fields, sustaining dual-shape input handling.
findings.suggestion: Split compatibility handling into explicit legacy input unions with strict mutual-exclusion validation, emit warnings/errors for mixed-mode payloads, and progressively narrow/remove deprecated fields per removal targets.

findings.summary: Legacy root hook paths remain as full implementations alongside newer domain paths, creating split ownership and boundary drift.
evidence: Both assigned root files are concrete implementations, not compatibility re-exports.

Equivalent files exist in `src/shared/hooks/async/useAsyncOperation.ts` and `src/shared/hooks/media/useBackgroundThumbnailGenerator.ts` with behaviorally meaningful API drift (`handleError` vs `reportRuntimeError`).
Keeping two live implementation trees for the same hook capabilities makes import location a historical accident rather than a stable package contract.
findings.suggestion: Pick one canonical hook namespace (`shared/hooks/async`, `shared/hooks/media`) and convert root paths to temporary facades with explicit deprecation metadata and CI enforcement, then remove the duplicate implementations after codemodding imports.

findings.summary: Hook tests are duplicated across generic and domain-specific folders, and the legacy copy still targets stale module boundaries.
evidence: Assigned generic tests have matching counterparts under `src/shared/hooks/__tests__/media/` and `src/shared/hooks/__tests__/ui-image/`.
`src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useBackgroundThumbnailGenerator.test.ts` mocks `@/shared/lib/errorHandler` (deprecated path) while the domain copy mocks `@/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError`.
This reintroduces the previously auto-resolved ownership issue by keeping both old and new test homes active.
findings.suggestion: Retain only the domain-owned test locations, delete generic duplicates, and add a lint rule preventing new top-level `src/shared/hooks/__tests__/*.test.ts` files unless they are truly cross-domain.

findings.summary: Top-level `shared/components` mixes app-shell and feature-specific components, reducing architectural predictability.
evidence: `AppErrorBoundary` is an app-level recovery boundary intended to wrap the whole application.
`AspectRatioSelector` is tied to generation controls and has an equivalent location under `src/shared/components/generation-controls/AspectRatioSelector.tsx`.
Keeping both in root `shared/components` obscures whether that directory is for primitives, app shell, or feature UI.
findings.suggestion: Define explicit component layer contracts (for example `shared/components/primitives`, `app/shell`, `features/*/components`) and move these files to their owning layers with thin compatibility exports during migration.

findings.summary: Global error presenter wiring still creates hidden runtime coupling via mutable shared notifier state.
evidence: `setErrorPresenter()` writes process-global notifier configuration through `configureErrorNotifier`, so behavior of `handleError()` depends on external registration order and singleton state.
README governance sections do not define ownership/lifecycle contract for this global presenter path, leaving cross-module coupling implicit.
findings.suggestion: Replace global setter wiring with an explicit dependency boundary (e.g., presenter passed through app bootstrap context/provider and consumed by error APIs) and document ownership/initialization order as a first-class runtime contract.

findings.summary: Top-level governance docs omit active error-handling deprecation contracts present in runtime code.
evidence: handleError.ts contains `@deprecationOwner runtime-foundation` and `@removalTarget 2026-06-30` for `reportRuntimeError` and `RuntimeErrorOptions`.
README compatibility/governance sections cover Supabase, shots shim, and cn contract, but do not track or enforce this error-handling deprecation scope.
findings.suggestion: Add an explicit 'Error handling deprecations' governance subsection in README and include the same owner/removal target plus check command linkage used for other compatibility contracts.

findings.summary: Error-handling deprecation contracts remain metadata-only and are not structured for direct migration-oracle enforcement.
evidence: `reportRuntimeError`/`RuntimeErrorOptions` deprecation policy is only in JSDoc annotations; unlike legacy Supabase governance constants, there is no exported contract constant set for date/owner/budget checks.
README lists quality gates for other migration contracts but no dedicated check is declared for these error-handling deprecations.
findings.suggestion: Promote error-handling deprecation metadata to exported governance constants and wire a check (plus contract test) that fails when removal date/budget policy drifts, matching the Supabase facade governance pattern.

findings.summary: Shot editor controllers still mix unrelated responsibilities behind oversized interfaces.
evidence: `UseEditingControllerParams` combines name editing, audio, structure-video, and join-clip concerns and returns a large mixed object (`useEditingController.ts:5`, `:55`, `:81`, `:92`, `:133`, `:146`, `:158`).

`UseGenerationControllerParams` has a broad cross-domain input surface (prompt/motion/model/join/stitch/UI loading/persistence callbacks), then mainly forwards the same values into `useGenerateBatch` (`useGenerationController.ts:7`, `:199`).
Parent controller passes a large fan-in object into each hook, confirming orchestration coupling at callsite (`useShotEditorController.ts:326`, `:439`).
findings.suggestion: Split the controller layer by workflow boundary (e.g., `useShotMediaEditing`, `useJoinConfig`, `useBatchGeneration`) and pass stable domain objects (prompt settings, join settings, motion settings) rather than flattened scalar lists. Keep each hook responsible for one workflow and expose narrow command-style APIs.

findings.summary: Lightbox layers still re-bundle and re-slice props without reducing conceptual load.
evidence: `MediaLightbox` builds `navigation`, `shotWorkflow`, `features`, `actions`, and `videoProps` via `useMemo` wrappers over incoming props (`MediaLightbox.tsx:80-146`).

`useLightboxWorkflowProps` re-composes these grouped objects into `workflowBarProps`, then spreads them again into `workflowControlsProps`, then into `layoutProps` (`useLightboxWorkflowProps.ts:91`, `:124`, `:133`).
`useLightboxStateAssembly` adds additional memoized slice wrappers that return mostly unchanged fields (`useLightboxStateAssembly.ts:4`, `:33`, `:65`).
findings.suggestion: Define a single lightbox view-model contract built once at the feature boundary (image/video shared adapter), and pass that model directly to layout/render layers. Remove pass-through grouping/slicing hooks that do not validate, translate, or enforce invariants.

findings.summary: Image form submission still carries a large data clump and duplicates param-building behavior.
evidence: `UseFormSubmissionProps` and `FormStateSnapshot` define wide overlapping payloads (`types.ts:13`, `:33`) that are copied into refs and forwarded through multiple hooks (`useFormSubmission.ts:50`, `:73`, `:93`).
`useTaskParamsBuilder` builds `referenceParams` and `buildBatchTaskParams` using shared submission inputs (`useTaskParamsBuilder.ts:71`, `:89`).
`useSubmitHandler` independently rebuilds the same `referenceParams` + `buildBatchTaskParams` flow in automated mode (`useSubmitHandler.ts:92`, `:95`), creating divergence risk.
findings.suggestion: Introduce one submission-domain adapter (e.g., `buildSubmissionPlan(formState, mode, prompts)`), and make both managed and automated paths call that shared adapter. Replace wide prop threading with a typed `SubmissionContext` object owned by the form submission module.

findings.summary: Task detail UI components still embed payload normalization and legacy fallback resolution.
evidence: `CharacterAnimateDetails` resolves each field by probing three payload shapes (`parsedParams`, `orchestrator_details`, `full_orchestrator_payload`) inside render component logic (`CharacterAnimateDetails.tsx:19-25`).

`TaskDetailsModal` derives input images from multiple fallback paths (`input_images`, `full_orchestrator_payload.input_image_paths_resolved`, `input_image_paths_resolved`) and cleans URL quoting inline in the modal (`TaskDetailsModal.tsx:74-99`).
`ImageEditTaskDetails` also performs local fallback adaptation between provided inputs and derived params (`ImageEditTaskDetails.tsx:17-19`), reinforcing per-component data adaptation.
findings.suggestion: Create a centralized task-details normalization layer (e.g., `normalizeTaskDetailsPayload(task)`), version/fallback once there, and have UI components consume a stable normalized view model with no payload-shape probing in render.
